name: Build a weekly summary with MySQL and Telegram in real time

parameters.text:
=Summarize these KPIs for leadership in 5 bullet points:
{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}
=✅ Build a weekly summary with MySQL and Telegram in real time — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 9:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Gather from MySQL

### Gather from MySQL

Selects rows from the MySQL table (`returnAll`) — the read side of the store.

Ports: ①→ Blend Sources · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Gather API Numbers

GET https://api.example.com/v1/emails — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Blend Sources · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Gather from Google Sheets

Reads every row from 'Email Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Blend Sources · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Blend Sources

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Roll Up KPIs

### Roll Up KPIs

Group-by + aggregate (like a pivot table) — turns raw rows into KPI numbers.

Next: Write Executive Summary (AI)

### Write Executive Summary (AI)

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Ports: ①→ Compose HTML Report · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Write Executive Summary (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Compose HTML Report

Custom JavaScript: Build an HTML digest table from the aggregated rows

Next: Send Report via Telegram

### Send Report via Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Load Previous Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Email Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per email (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Sort Emails Newest First

### Sort Emails Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Guard Non-Empty from

### Guard Non-Empty from

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 50 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#inbox-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#inbox-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

# Build a weekly summary with MySQL and Telegram in real time

**Pattern:** Scheduled Digest · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Build a weekly summary with MySQL and Telegram in real time. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual auto-responders is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Gather from MySQL → Blend Sources → Roll Up KPIs → Write Executive Summary (AI) → Compose HTML Report → Send Report via Telegram → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to scheduled digest without manual effort
- Anyone automating auto-responders in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; MySQL connection; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

KPIs are rolled up with Summarize (exact group-by + aggregate) before any AI touches the data — asking an LLM to 'figure out the numbers' from raw records is slower, costlier, and less reliable than doing exact arithmetic in n8n and only asking the model to narrate numbers that are already correct.

## Practice exercises

About as deep as this pattern goes — extend it:

1. Add a second data source and watch it blend into the same report via Merge.
2. Toggle the AI executive-summary step on and off and compare the report's usefulness.
3. Change the KPI grouping field and see how the pivot reshapes.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your auto-responders process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
